behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs can appear in module handoff (file discovery -> filename parsing -> execution sequence), not just inside one sort helper. REAL-INFRA: no explicit OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local filesystem/DB. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text does not require external contract verification or cross-process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp migrations directory with out-of-order filenames (e.g., 001, 010, 002), run the runner against a local test DB, then assert applied order from `_migrations` rows (or logged execution order) is 001 -> 002 -> 010.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: idempotency depends on persisted state across runs (`_migrations` read/write + executor skip logic). A unit test of skip logic alone can miss wiring/state bugs. REAL-INFRA: no explicit need for real subprocess/network. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior is local DB state orchestration, not external protocol contract.
behaviors.test_description: Run runner once on a local test DB with a migration that mutates data, run it again, then assert (a) migration appears once in `_migrations` and (b) mutation effect was not duplicated.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure handling spans execution, transaction, and runner control flow; unit-only can miss rollback/stop wiring issues. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with a local real DB transaction; no explicit subprocess/OS contract needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external API/HTTP/CLI contract checks.
behaviors.test_description: Use migrations where one file performs a write then fails mid-migration, followed by another migration file. Run runner against local test DB, then verify failed migration effects are absent (rollback), `_migrations` excludes the failed migration, and subsequent migration was not applied.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires concurrent execution from separate processes, which is OS/subprocess scheduling + DB locking/isolation behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-process or mocked concurrency can hide race conditions and lock timing failures. Rejected Integration because text explicitly says "separate processes" and exact-once under real contention.
behaviors.test_description: Start two runner processes simultaneously against the same real test DB (barrier start), wait for both to finish, then assert each migration filename appears exactly once in `_migrations` and schema/data effects are present exactly once.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the `_migrations` table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: startup bootstrap ordering spans metadata-table initialization and migration execution modules. Unit tests on either step alone can miss ordering defects. REAL-INFRA: local DB is sufficient; no required subprocess/network contract checks. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because no explicit external contract or cross-process requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Against an empty local test DB, run runner with at least one migration; verify `_migrations` exists and contains the first migration record only after table creation, and first migration succeeds without bootstrap-order errors.
